mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate near zero reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and lowers economy-wide borrowing costs, which increases equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return rate during low-rate months is not significantly above 50% (binomial test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.5% indicates persistent labor market slack that suppresses wage inflation expectations and extends the duration of accommodative monetary policy, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated retail sales and consumer prices signal demand-pull inflation that compresses corporate real margins and increases the probability of tighter future monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL achieves a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or joint high readings fail to coincide with negative 20-day returns in greater than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation erodes the real value of future corporate earnings streams and raises discount rates through tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of CPIAUCSL level remains above -0.03 over any 2-year window, or high inflation months show positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below the 2017 baseline index level of 100 signals underutilized manufacturing capacity and weak goods demand, which weighs on industrial earnings and drags the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP remaining below 20,000 billion chained 2017 dollars indicates the economy is operating below potential output, which keeps monetary policy accommodative and equity risk premiums compressed, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered observations produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher levels of nonfarm payrolls reflect expanded aggregate wage income and household consumption capacity, which translate into positive revenue surprises for consumer-facing firms and lift the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS level or 1-month change falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency does not exceed 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.5 million units signal robust residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials, appliances, and mortgage credit, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered periods produce negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of instances over any 3-year walk-forward window.
